The codebase is at version 2.174 (commit: 6d2f8951). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: Answer comparison not shown when type answer into the card is enabled ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? - Enable `Advanced → Type answer into the card` - Review a card that requires typing the answer. ### Expected behaviour After clicking "Show Answer", the comparision of the typed and actual answers should be shown. It is the case when the above-mentioned Advanced option is disabled. ### Actual behaviour Only the correct answer is shown. ### Debug info ```text 2.17 beta 4 ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share?  279→    private fun selectDeckFromExtra() {
 280→        val extras = intent.extras
 281→        if (extras == null || !extras.containsKey("deckId")) {
 282→            // deckId is not set, load default
 283→            return
 284→        }
 285→        val did = extras.getLong("deckId", Long.MIN_VALUE)
 286→        Timber.d("selectDeckFromExtra() with deckId = %d", did)
 287→
 288→        // deckId does not exist, load default
 289→        if (getColUnsafe.decks.get(did) == null) {
 290→            Timber.w("selectDeckFromExtra() deckId '%d' doesn't exist", did)
 291→            return
 292→        }
 293→        // Select the deck
 294→        getColUnsafe.decks.select(did)
 295→    }

 309→    override fun onCollectionLoaded(col: Collection) {
 310→        super.onCollectionLoaded(col)
 311→        if (Intent.ACTION_VIEW == intent.action) {
 312→            Timber.d("onCreate() :: received Intent with action = %s", intent.action)
 313→            selectDeckFromExtra()
 314→        }
 315→        // Load the first card and start reviewing. Uses the answer card
 316→        // task to load a card, but since we send null
 317→        // as the card to answer, no card will be answered.
 318→        prefWhiteboard = MetaDB.getWhiteboardState(this, parentDid)
 319→        if (prefWhiteboard) {
 320→            // DEFECT: Slight inefficiency here, as we set the database using these methods
 321→            val whiteboardVisibility = MetaDB.getWhiteboardVisibility(this, parentDid)
 322→            setWhiteboardEnabledState(true)
 323→            setWhiteboardVisibility(whiteboardVisibility)
 324→            toggleStylus = MetaDB.getWhiteboardStylusState(this, parentDid)
 325→            whiteboard!!.toggleStylus = toggleStylus
 326→        }
 327→        launchCatchingTask {
 328→            withCol { startTimebox() }
 329→            updateCardAndRedraw()
 330→        }
 331→        disableDrawerSwipeOnConflicts()
 332→
 333→        // Set full screen/immersive mode if needed
 334→        if (prefFullscreenReview) {
 335→            setFullScreen(this)
 336→        }
 337→        setRenderWorkaround(this)
 338→    }

 993→        if (counts.count() != 0) {

1095→    private fun runStateMutationHook() {
1096→        val state = queueState ?: return
1097→        if (state.customSchedulingJs.isEmpty()) {
1098→            statesMutated = true
1099→            return
1100→        }
1101→        val key = customSchedulingKey
1102→        val js = state.customSchedulingJs
1103→        webView?.evaluateJavascript(
1104→            """
1105→        anki.mutateNextCardStates('$key', async (states, customData, ctx) => { $js })
1106→            .catch(err => { console.log(err); window.location.href = "state-mutation-error:"; });
1107→"""
1108→        ) { result ->
1109→            if ("null" == result) {
1110→                // eval failed, usually a syntax error
1111→                // Note, we get "null" (string) and not null
1112→                statesMutated = true
1113→            }
1114→        }
1115→    }

1357→            // LOW_PROFILE, HIDE_NAVIGATION, or FULLSCREEN flags are set.
1358→            val visible = flags and View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_HIDE_NAVIGATION == 0
1359→            Timber.d("System UI visibility change. Visible: %b", visible)

1398→    override suspend fun handlePostRequest(uri: String, bytes: ByteArray): ByteArray {
1399→        return if (uri.startsWith(ANKI_PREFIX)) {
1400→            when (val methodName = uri.substring(ANKI_PREFIX.length)) {
1401→                "getSchedulingStatesWithContext" -> getSchedulingStatesWithContext()
1402→                "setSchedulingStates" -> setSchedulingStates(bytes)
1403→                else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled request: $methodName")
1404→            }
1405→        } else if (uri.startsWith(ANKIDROID_JS_PREFIX)) {
1406→            jsApi.handleJsApiRequest(
1407→                uri.substring(ANKIDROID_JS_PREFIX.length),
1408→                bytes,
1409→                returnDefaultValues = false
1410→            )
1411→        } else {
1412→            throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled request: $uri")
1413→        }
1414→    }

1416→    private fun getSchedulingStatesWithContext(): ByteArray {
1417→        val state = queueState ?: return ByteArray(0)
1418→        return state.schedulingStatesWithContext().toBuilder()
1419→            .mergeStates(
1420→                state.states.toBuilder().mergeCurrent(
1421→                    state.states.current.toBuilder()
1422→                        .setCustomData(state.topCard.toBackendCard().customData).build()
1423→                ).build()
1424→            )
1425→            .build()
1426→            .toByteArray()
1427→    }
1428→
1429→    private fun setSchedulingStates(bytes: ByteArray): ByteArray {
1430→        val state = queueState
1431→        if (state == null) {
1432→            statesMutated = true
1433→            return ByteArray(0)
1434→        }
1435→        val req = SetSchedulingStatesRequest.parseFrom(bytes)
1436→        if (req.key == customSchedulingKey) {
1437→            state.states = req.states
1438→        }
1439→        statesMutated = true
1440→        return ByteArray(0)
1441→    }

1443→    private fun createWhiteboard() {
1444→        whiteboard = createInstance(this, true, this)
1445→
1446→        // We use the pen color of the selected deck at the time the whiteboard is enabled.
1447→        // This is how all other whiteboard settings are
1448→        val whiteboardPenColor = MetaDB.getWhiteboardPenColor(this, parentDid).fromPreferences()
1449→        if (whiteboardPenColor != null) {
1450→            whiteboard!!.penColor = whiteboardPenColor
1451→        }
1452→        whiteboard!!.setOnPaintColorChangeListener(object : OnPaintColorChangeListener {
1453→            override fun onPaintColorChange(color: Int?) {
1454→                MetaDB.storeWhiteboardPenColor(this@Reviewer, parentDid, !currentTheme.isNightMode, color)
1455→            }
1456→        })
1457→        whiteboard!!.setOnTouchListener { v: View, event: MotionEvent? ->
1458→            if (event == null) return@setOnTouchListener false
1459→            // If the whiteboard is currently drawing, and triggers the system UI to show, we want to continue drawing.
1460→            if (!whiteboard!!.isCurrentlyDrawing && (
1461→                !showWhiteboard || (
1462→                    prefFullscreenReview &&
1463→                        isImmersiveSystemUiVisible(this@Reviewer)
1464→                    )
1465→                )
1466→            ) {
1467→                // Bypass whiteboard listener when it's hidden or fullscreen immersive mode is temporarily suspended
1468→                v.performClick()
1469→                return@setOnTouchListener gestureDetector!!.onTouchEvent(event)
1470→            }
1471→            whiteboard!!.handleTouchEvent(event)
1472→        }
1473→    }

 615→                    showThemedToast(

 617→                        this.getText(R.string.multimedia_editor_audio_view_create_failed).toString(),
 618→                        true
 619→                    )
 620→                }
 621→                isAudioUIInitialized = true
 622→            }
 623→            micToolBarLayer.visibility = View.VISIBLE
 624→        }

 972→    private fun updateScreenCounts() {
 973→        val queue = queueState ?: return
 974→        super.updateActionBar()
 975→        val actionBar = supportActionBar
 976→        val counts = queue.counts
 977→        if (actionBar != null) {
 978→            if (prefShowETA) {
 979→                launchCatchingTask {
 980→                    eta = withCol { sched.eta(counts, false) }

 982→                }
 983→            }
 984→        }
 985→        newCount = SpannableString(counts.new.toString())
 986→        lrnCount = SpannableString(counts.lrn.toString())
 987→        revCount = SpannableString(counts.rev.toString())
 988→        if (prefHideDueCount) {
 989→            revCount = SpannableString("???")
 990→        }
 991→        // if this code is run as a card is being answered, currentCard may be non-null but
 992→        // the queues may be empty - we can't call countIdx() in such a case
 993→        if (counts.count() != 0) {
 994→            when (queue.countsIndex) {
 995→                Counts.Queue.NEW -> newCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, newCount!!.length, 0)
 996→                Counts.Queue.LRN -> lrnCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, lrnCount!!.length, 0)
 997→                Counts.Queue.REV -> revCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, revCount!!.length, 0)
 998→            }
 999→        }
1000→        textBarNew.text = newCount
1001→        textBarLearn.text = lrnCount
1002→        textBarReview.text = revCount
1003→    }

1095→    private fun runStateMutationHook() {
1096→        val state = queueState ?: return
1097→        if (state.customSchedulingJs.isEmpty()) {
1098→            statesMutated = true
1099→            return
1100→        }
1101→        val key = customSchedulingKey
1102→        val js = state.customSchedulingJs
1103→        webView?.evaluateJavascript(
1104→            """
1105→        anki.mutateNextCardStates('$key', async (states, customData, ctx) => { $js })
1106→            .catch(err => { console.log(err); window.location.href = "state-mutation-error:"; });
1107→"""
1108→        ) { result ->
1109→            if ("null" == result) {
1110→                // eval failed, usually a syntax error
1111→                // Note, we get "null" (string) and not null
1112→                statesMutated = true
1113→            }
1114→        }
1115→    }

153→    /**
154→     * Product Flavors are used for Amazon App Store and Google Play Store.
155→     * This is because we cannot use Camera Permissions in Amazon App Store (for FireTv etc...)
156→     * Therefore, different AndroidManifest for Camera Permissions is used in Amazon flavor.
157→     *
158→     * This flavor block must stay in sync with the same block in testlib/build.gradle.kts
159→     */
160→    flavorDimensions += "appStore"
161→    productFlavors {
162→        create('play') {
163→            dimension "appStore"
164→        }
165→        create('amazon') {
166→            dimension "appStore"
167→        }
168→        // A 'full' build has no restrictions on storage/camera. Distributed on GitHub/F-Droid
169→        create('full') {
170→            dimension "appStore"
171→        }
172→    }

174→    /**
175→     * Set this to true to create five separate APKs instead of one:
176→     *   - 2 APKs that only work on ARM/ARM64 devices
177→     *   - 2 APKs that only works on x86/x86_64 devices
178→     *   - a universal APK that works on all devices
179→     * The advantage is the size of most APKs is reduced by about 2.5MB.
180→     * Upload all the APKs to the Play Store and people will download
181→     * the correct one based on the CPU architecture of their device.
182→     */
183→    def enableSeparateBuildPerCPUArchitecture = true
184→
185→    splits {
186→        abi {
187→            reset()
188→            enable enableSeparateBuildPerCPUArchitecture
189→            //universalApk enableUniversalApk  // set in debug + release config blocks above
190→            include "armeabi-v7a", "x86", "arm64-v8a", "x86_64"
191→        }
192→    }
193→    // applicationVariants are e.g. debug, release
194→    applicationVariants.configureEach { variant ->
195→        // We want the same version stream for all ABIs in debug but for release we can split them
196→        if (variant.buildType.name == 'release') {
197→            variant.outputs.configureEach { output ->
198→
199→                // For each separate APK per architecture, set a unique version code as described here:
200→                // https://developer.android.com/studio/build/configure-apk-splits.html
201→                def versionCodes = ["armeabi-v7a": 1, "x86": 2, "arm64-v8a": 3, "x86_64": 4]
202→                def outputFile = output.outputFile
203→                if (outputFile != null && outputFile.name.endsWith('.apk')) {
204→                    def abi = output.getFilter("ABI")
205→                    if (abi != null) {  // null for the universal-debug, universal-release variants
206→                        //  From: https://developer.android.com/studio/publish/versioning#appversioning
207→                        //  "Warning: The greatest value Google Play allows for versionCode is 2100000000"
208→                        //  AnkiDroid versionCodes have a budget 8 digits (through AnkiDroid 9)
209→                        //  This style does ABI version code ranges with the 9th digit as 0-4.
210→                        //  This consumes ~20% of the version range space, w/50 years of versioning at our major-version pace
211→                        output.versionCodeOverride =
212→                                // ex:  321200106 = 3 * 100000000 + 21200106
213→                                versionCodes.get(abi) * 100000000 + defaultConfig.versionCode
214→                    }
215→                }
216→            }
217→        }
218→    }

260→// Issue 11078 - some emulators run, but run zero tests, and still report success
261→tasks.register('assertNonzeroAndroidTests') {
262→    doLast {
263→        // androidTest currently creates one .xml file per emulator with aggregate results in this dir
264→        File folder = file("./build/outputs/androidTest-results/connected/flavors/play")
265→        File[] listOfFiles = folder.listFiles({ d, f -> f ==~ /.*.xml/ } as FilenameFilter)
266→        for (File file : listOfFiles) {
267→            // The aggregate results file currently contains a line with this pattern holding test count
268→            String[] matches = file.readLines().findAll { it.contains('<testsuite') }
269→            if (matches.length != 1) {
270→                throw new GradleScriptException("Unable to determine count of tests executed for " + file.name + ". Regex pattern out of date?", null)
271→            }
272→            if (!(matches[0] ==~ /.* tests="\d+" .*/) || matches[0].contains('tests="0"')) {
273→                throw new GradleScriptException("androidTest executed 0 tests for " + file.name + " - Probably a bug with the emulator. Try another image.", null)
274→            }
275→        }
276→    }
277→}
278→afterEvaluate {
279→    tasks.named('connectedPlayDebugAndroidTest').configure { finalizedBy('assertNonzeroAndroidTests') }
